title: Aurek-class tactical strikefighter
content:

separatists]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' *Numerous civilian organizations }} The '''''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighter''', also known as an '''A-wing''', was a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], several models of which were used in the [[Old Sith Wars]], the [[Great Galactic War]],{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 3: The Return of the Mandalorians|int=Timeline 3: The Return of the Mandalorians}} the [[New Sith Wars]],''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' and even until the final generation of the Republic.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Characteristics== [[File:Aurekwingsclosed.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A squadron of Aureks on [[Serroco]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]]] The Aurek fighter was a 9.2-meter-long delta-shaped starfighter armed with two heavy [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and two [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launchers, capable of holding six torpedoes. The fighter was capable of considerable acceleration and maneuvering, and had variable-geometry wings. They were easily refuelable and rearmable, thus driving down costs. Although the Aurek fighters saw combat throughout a span of 4,000 years as the Republic's main "hyperspace snub," they underwent extensive upgrades and revisions from one era to the next. ==History== Following the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the Republic entered an era of restoration. During this period, the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] requested a "Generation Tomorrow Starfighter" to replace their aging [[Star Saber XC-01 starfighter|Star Saber XC-01]]. The Aurek was the winning design.
[[File:Aurek_tactical_strikefighter.jpg|thumb|220px|An ''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighter destroys a [[Sith fighter]]]]
Aurek fighter models were used by the Republic during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] ([[3976 BBY]]–[[3960 BBY]]),[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] the [[Jedi Civil War]] (c. [[3956 BBY]]), the [[First Jedi Purge]] (c. [[3951 BBY]]), the [[Great Galactic War]] ([[3681 BBY]]–[[3653 BBY]]) and the [[New Sith Wars]] (c. [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]] – c. [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]). They also saw service in the [[Onderonian Space Force|Onderonian navy]] and in civilian squadrons, due to a naval policy of reselling damaged fighters and buying new ones rather than repairing them. Several squadrons of Aurek fighters comprised part of the Republic fleet that attacked the [[Star Forge]] during the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]]. A number of these ships were used by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] to land on board the Star Forge during the decisive strike against [[Darth Malak]]'s Sith Empire. Later, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vaklu]]'s fleet, which blockaded [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] during the Sith Civil War, included fighters of this type, six of which attacked the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' when it entered the [[Japrael system/Legends|Onderon system]] only to be destroyed by the ship's dorsal [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turret in the ensuing dogfight. Aurek fighters continued to see use in the [[Great Galactic War]] and, later, the [[New Sith Wars]]. [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]] could hold a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of Aurek fighters during the New Sith Wars era. The contemporary [[Corellian Colonizer]] [[Praxeum ship/Legends|Praxeum ships]] carried at least 12 Aureks each.[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] The design would still see usage later, with models in use until the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], though as the product of Outer Rim engineers rather than shipyard design teams. ==Behind the scenes== As with most craft seen in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', this ship was initially unnamed. Eventually, ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' tentatively identified the ship as the A-wing. However, with the release of ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', the ship was renamed to become the Aurek fighter, and [[Leland Chee]] confirmed it was the same ship.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=0960#15882292|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-11-03|time=5:05 PM}} As [[Aurek/Legends|Aurek]] is the first letter of [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]], the alphabet of [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic]], the new name is considered to hold the same meaning. The sound effect used for the Aurek fighter's engines is borrowed from that of the [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Aurek-class tactical strikefighters}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights
